L1.area: Browser launch request processing and URL handling

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where a crafted URL argument that fails standard URL parsing is silently accepted as a raw local file path without any validation or sanitization. This allows an attacker to access arbitrary files on the system by providing a specially crafted input string to the browser-opening functionality.

L3.area: URL-to-target type conversion and browser launch entry point
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where input strings provided to the browser-opening API that fail URL parsing are silently converted into raw file path targets without validation. An attacker can supply a crafted string that, instead of being rejected or properly converted to a file URL, is directly used as a local file path, allowing access to arbitrary files on the system.

ground_truth.reason: The From<&str> implementation for TargetType accepts any string that fails URL parsing as a raw file path without validation, allowing attackers to access arbitrary files by supplying crafted URL inputs to the browser-opening API.
